questions.question: Why was the Constituent Assembly formed?
questions.answer: To draft the Constitution of India.

questions.question: What was one major challenge India faced after independence?
Who was the first Prime Minister of India?
What does federalism mean?
questions.answer: Division of powers between central and state governments.

questions.question: Why were states reorganized in 1956?
questions.answer: Mainly on the basis of language.

questions.question: Which commission recommended the reorganization of states?
What was the aim of the Five-Year Plans?
Which sector received priority in the First Five-Year Plan?
Which plan emphasized heavy industries?
Who is known as the father of the Green Revolution in India?

Name one state that benefited greatly from the Green Revolution.
What does national integration promote?
Which institution ensures free and fair elections?
What is democracy?
What does urbanization mean?
questions.answer: Growth of cities due to migration and development.

questions.question: Why did urbanization increase after independence?
questions.answer: Due to industrial development and job opportunities.

questions.question: Which organization manages India's space research?
What is economic planning?
questions.answer: A system of planning economic development through government policies.

questions.question: Which body makes laws in India?
What does national development refer to?
Name one factor that improved agricultural productivity.
What role does education play in development?
questions.answer: It helps create skilled and knowledgeable citizens.

questions.question: What is the role of the Supreme Court?
questions.answer: To protect the Constitution and interpret laws.

questions.question: What does 'unity in diversity' represent in India?
questions.answer: Different cultures living together in one nation.
